'''Dborian''', also known as '''Lazerian V''', was the fifth [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Lazerian system/Legends|Lazerian system]], [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Lazerian (star)|Lazerian]].<ref name="TSoK" /> It was located within the [[Kira sector/Legends|Kira sector]]<ref name="TEA-OC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="TEA" /> Along with the rest of the Lazerian [[Star system/Legends|system]], Dborian lay on the [[Harrin Trade Corridor]] and [[Kira Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]].<ref name="TSoK" />

The planet's surface was barren and rocky, and the world had two [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. It had a Type II [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]<ref name="TSoK" /> in which the use of [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]] was suggested.<ref name="Planets" /> At some point, Dborian was surveyed by the [[Lazerian (people)|inhabitants]] of the planet [[Lazerian IV]]. The world eventually came to serve as a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Colonist/Legends|colony]] but was otherwise uninhabited.<ref name="TSoK" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dborian was created by [[John Terra]] and introduced in [[Twin Stars of Kira (sourcebook)|''Twin Stars of Kira'']], a [[1993]] supplement to [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the planet's name was misspelled as "Dhorian" on one instance.<ref name="TSoK" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Lazerian system, and therefore Dborian, in grid square N-15.<ref name="TEA" />
